summary:
The process of cementum resorption happens at a quicker rate when contrasted against typical bone tissue dissolution; studies highlight greater involvement of osteoclasts leading to efficient destruction within dental structures' periodontal ligaments areas specifically affected during extraction procedures or other pathologies impacting these regions directly. Thus, confirming cementum’s susceptibility for rapid reabsorption over standard bony elements subjected similarly adverse physiological states. 

The summary captures two critical aspects highlighted above—enhanced osteoclast action contributing significantly toward swifter cementum disintegration versus slower progression noted among bones—and emphasizes their relevance concerning differential responses between both types of connective tissues facing analogous medical challenges like those encountered around extracted teeth roots etc.,
